rationale: Amphiphilic APIs often benefit from a balanced polar-apolar local environment and strong local binder contacts.
Cellulose-ether aqueous route that usually tolerates polar APIs while preserving a printable semi-solid path.
Flexible polar APIs often sit well in cellulose-ether routes with a controlled volatile co-solvent component.
Hydrogen-bond-rich peptide-like polymer comparator for flexible polar APIs.
Polysaccharide gel-network comparator for very water-affine APIs.
Flexible solvent-rich comparator for amphiphilic APIs.
Simple vehicle-like comparator for flexible polar APIs.
Cellulose-ether route is a strong first screen for acidic or anionic APIs in printable semi-solid contexts.
PVP remains a useful comparator for acidic APIs because it can still offer strong local H-bonding and dispersion support.
An anionic polysaccharide gel route is worth screening when the goal includes robust gel-network behavior.
General fallback cellulose-ether route for acidic or anionic APIs.
Pectin plus glycerol-water is a softer gel-route comparator for acidic APIs and drying-sensitive systems.
